things.name: Historic Inner City (Paramaribo Old Town)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage Dutch colonial city with a unique Caribbean wooden architecture

things.name: Saint Peter and Paul Cathedral
things.description: One of the largest wooden structures in the Western Hemisphere built by Dutch Redemptorists

things.name: Central Market (Centrale Markt)
things.description: Multi-ethnic market reflecting Suriname's extraordinary diversity - Creole, Javanese, Chinese

things.name: Joden Savanne Jewish Cemetery
things.description: Ruins of the oldest synagogue in the Western Hemisphere built by Sephardic Jews in 1671

things.name: Fort Zeelandia Museum
things.description: 17th-century Dutch fort on the Suriname River now housing historical collections

things.name: Brownsberg Nature Park
things.description: Rainforest park above the Brokopondo reservoir with howler monkeys and toucans

things.name: Neveh Shalom Synagogue
things.description: Active 1834 synagogue with a sand floor - oldest in continuous use in the Americas

things.name: Commewijne District Plantation Cruise
things.description: River boat to former Dutch coffee and sugar plantations with dolphins

things.name: Central Suriname Nature Reserve
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage 1.6 million hectares of pristine Amazon rainforest

things.name: Pom Cassava & Chicken Dish
things.description: Suriname's national dish - pomtajer root with chicken, lemon and spices
